events.eventText: Julius Caesar is stabbed to death
events.description: 23 wounds on the Ides of March

events.eventText: Vesuvius buries Pompeii in ash
events.description: Preserved the city so well bread was found in ovens

events.eventText: Genghis Khan is proclaimed Great Khan
events.description: United the Mongol tribes into the largest land empire

events.eventText: Gutenberg prints the first Bible
events.description: Movable type launched a revolution in knowledge

events.eventText: Isaac Newton publishes Principia
events.description: Laid the foundations of classical mechanics

events.eventText: Storming of the Bastille in Paris
events.description: The event that ignited the French Revolution

events.eventText: The Wright Brothers fly at Kitty Hawk
events.description: The first powered flight lasted just 12 seconds

events.eventText: Neil Armstrong walks on the Moon
events.description: The mission computer had less power than a calculator

events.eventText: The Berlin Wall is torn down
events.description: East Germans flooded through after a confused press conference

events.eventText: ChatGPT launches to the public
events.description: Reached 100 million users faster than any app in history

events.eventText: Alhazen completes the Book of Optics
events.description: Founded experimental science through work with light

events.eventText: Copernicus publishes his sun-centred model
events.description: Published on his deathbed to avoid controversy

events.eventText: Galileo first observes Jupiter's moons
events.description: Proved not everything orbits the Earth

events.eventText: Edward Jenner tests the smallpox vaccine
events.description: Used cowpox to create immunity in a young boy

events.eventText: Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species
events.description: Sold out its entire first print run in one day

events.eventText: Marie Curie isolates radium
events.description: Her notebooks are still too radioactive to handle

events.eventText: Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin
events.description: Found by accident when mould contaminated a dish

events.eventText: Watson and Crick describe DNA's structure
events.description: Built on crucial X-ray work by Rosalind Franklin

events.eventText: Dolly the sheep is born via cloning
events.description: Named after Dolly Parton

events.eventText: CRISPR gene editing is demonstrated
events.description: Allowed precise editing of DNA for the first time

events.eventText: Greeks defeat Persia at Marathon
events.description: A runner allegedly died delivering news of victory

events.eventText: Hannibal crosses the Alps with elephants
events.description: Lost nearly half his army on the mountain crossing

events.eventText: Harold II is killed at Hastings
events.description: Reportedly struck by an arrow to the eye

events.eventText: The Spanish Armada is scattered
events.description: Storms did as much damage as the English fleet

events.eventText: Napoleon is defeated at Waterloo
events.description: His final battle lasted less than a single day

events.eventText: Pickett's Charge fails at Gettysburg
events.description: The turning point of the American Civil War

events.eventText: The Somme offensive begins
events.description: Nearly 20,000 British soldiers died on the first day

events.eventText: Allied forces land on Normandy beaches
events.description: Over 150,000 troops crossed the English Channel

events.eventText: US helicopters evacuate Saigon
events.description: Evacuees airlifted from the embassy roof

events.eventText: Coalition forces invade Iraq
events.description: Baghdad fell within three weeks of the invasion

events.eventText: Leif Erikson lands in North America
events.description: Beat Columbus by nearly 500 years

events.eventText: Marco Polo arrives at Kublai Khan's court
events.description: Spent 17 years in the Mongol emperor's service

events.eventText: Columbus reaches the Caribbean
events.description: Believed he had reached Asia until his death

events.eventText: Magellan is killed in the Philippines
events.description: His crew completed the first circumnavigation without him

events.eventText: Captain Cook reaches Botany Bay
events.description: Aboriginal Australians had lived there for 65,000 years

events.eventText: Amundsen reaches the South Pole
events.description: Beat Robert Scott by 34 days

events.eventText: Hillary and Tenzing summit Everest
events.description: News reached London on the morning of the coronation

events.eventText: Yuri Gagarin orbits the Earth
events.description: The entire flight lasted just 108 minutes

events.eventText: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
events.description: Armstrong and Aldrin walked for just 2.5 hours

events.eventText: Voyager 1 enters interstellar space
events.description: Still transmitting from over 24 billion km away

events.eventText: Construction of the Parthenon begins
events.description: Built without mortar — precision-cut marble held it together

events.eventText: Work begins on Angkor Wat
events.description: Became the largest religious monument ever built

events.eventText: Dante completes The Divine Comedy
events.description: Wrote in Italian instead of Latin, shocking the literary world

events.eventText: Leonardo begins painting the Mona Lisa
events.description: Carried it with him for years, endlessly refining it

events.eventText: Shakespeare's Hamlet premieres in London
events.description: The longest of his plays at over 4,000 lines

events.eventText: Beethoven premieres his Ninth Symphony
events.description: Conducted while almost completely deaf

events.eventText: Lumiere brothers screen the first film
events.description: Audiences reportedly fled from an oncoming train

events.eventText: Picasso paints Guernica
events.description: Responding to the bombing of a Basque town

events.eventText: Tim Berners-Lee launches the first website
events.description: It explained what the World Wide Web was

events.eventText: Spotify launches in Sweden
events.description: Now has over 600 million users worldwide

events.eventText: Cyrus the Great captures Babylon
events.description: Created the first charter of human rights

events.eventText: Alexander the Great enters Egypt
events.description: Founded the city of Alexandria at age 24

events.eventText: Charlemagne is crowned Holy Roman Emperor
events.description: United most of Western Europe since the fall of Rome

events.eventText: The Aztecs found Tenochtitlan
events.description: Built on a lake island where Mexico City now stands

events.eventText: Constantinople falls to Ottoman forces
events.description: Ended over 1,000 years of the Byzantine Empire

events.eventText: Babur wins the Battle of Panipat
events.description: Founded the Mughal Empire in India

events.eventText: The East India Company receives its charter
events.description: Became the most powerful corporation in history

events.eventText: Napoleon crowns himself Emperor
events.description: Took the crown from the Pope and placed it himself

events.eventText: Queen Victoria is proclaimed Empress of India
events.description: The British Empire reached its greatest extent

events.eventText: Gorbachev resigns as the USSR dissolves
events.description: Stepped down on Christmas Day

events.eventText: Cai Lun presents papermaking to the Emperor
events.description: Replaced bamboo strips and silk for writing

events.eventText: Chinese alchemists first record gunpowder
events.description: Created while searching for an elixir of immortality

events.eventText: Gutenberg builds his printing press
events.description: Reduced the cost of books by roughly 80%

events.eventText: James Watt patents his steam engine
events.description: Kick-started the Industrial Revolution

events.eventText: Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone
events.description: Beat a rival application by just two hours

events.eventText: ENIAC, the first electronic computer, runs
events.description: Weighed 30 tonnes and filled an entire room

events.eventText: The first mobile phone call is made
events.description: Motorola's Martin Cooper called his rival to gloat

events.eventText: The World Wide Web goes public
events.description: Built by Tim Berners-Lee at CERN in Switzerland

events.eventText: The iPhone goes on sale
events.description: Steve Jobs introduced it as three products in one

events.eventText: SpaceX lands a rocket booster upright
events.description: Made reusable rockets a reality

events.eventText: Spartacus leads a slave revolt from Capua
events.description: Up to 120,000 escaped slaves fought the Roman army

events.eventText: King John seals the Magna Carta
events.description: Forced to accept limits on royal power

events.eventText: Martin Luther posts the 95 Theses
events.description: Sparked the Protestant Reformation across Europe

events.eventText: The US Declaration of Independence is signed
events.description: Most delegates signed on 2 August, not 4 July

events.eventText: Haiti declares independence
events.description: The only successful large-scale slave revolt in history

events.eventText: Marx and Engels publish The Communist Manifesto
events.description: Originally just a 23-page pamphlet

events.eventText: Tsar Nicholas II abdicates in a railway car
events.description: Ended 300 years of Romanov rule in Russia

events.eventText: India gains independence at midnight
events.description: Midnight transfer to avoid a bad astrological date

events.eventText: Fidel Castro enters Havana
events.description: He was just 32 years old

events.eventText: A Tunisian vendor's protest sparks the Arab Spring
events.description: Triggered uprisings across the Middle East

events.eventText: Siddhartha Gautama is born in Lumbini
events.description: Founded what became one of the world's major religions

events.eventText: Confucius begins teaching in the state of Lu
events.description: His ideas shaped East Asian culture for 2,500 years

events.eventText: Jesus of Nazareth is crucified in Jerusalem
events.description: The event that launched the world's largest religion

events.eventText: Muhammad's followers migrate to Medina
events.description: Year one of the Islamic calendar

events.eventText: Thomas Aquinas completes Summa Theologica
events.description: Reconciled Christian faith with Aristotelian reason

events.eventText: The King James Bible is first printed
events.description: Took 47 scholars seven years to complete

events.eventText: Voltaire publishes Candide
events.description: A savage satire on optimism, banned across Europe

events.eventText: Karl Marx publishes Das Kapital, Volume I
events.description: Spent most of his research time in the British Museum

events.eventText: Gandhi begins the Salt March
events.description: Walked 390 km to protest the British salt monopoly

events.eventText: Martin Luther King delivers his Dream speech
events.description: The most famous part was improvised on the spot

events.eventText: The Great Fire of London breaks out
events.description: Destroyed 13,200 houses but officially killed only six

events.eventText: A massive earthquake destroys Lisbon
events.description: Shook European philosophy — Voltaire wrote about it

events.eventText: The Irish potato blight first appears
events.description: Ireland's population has still never recovered

events.eventText: The Titanic strikes an iceberg and sinks
events.description: Only enough lifeboats for a third of those on board

events.eventText: Spanish Flu is first reported at a Kansas base
events.description: Infected a third of the world's population

events.eventText: The atomic bomb is dropped on Hiroshima
events.description: The bomber crew saw the flash from 600 km away

events.eventText: Reactor 4 at Chernobyl explodes
events.description: Created an exclusion zone that still exists today

events.eventText: A tsunami devastates the Indian Ocean coast
events.description: Waves reached 30 metres and hit 14 countries

events.eventText: The Deepwater Horizon oil rig explodes
events.description: Spilled 4.9 million barrels into the Gulf of Mexico

events.eventText: COVID-19 is declared a global pandemic
events.description: Led to the fastest vaccine development in history

events.eventText: The first coins are minted in Lydia
events.description: Made from a gold-silver alloy called electrum

events.eventText: The Medici Bank opens in Florence
events.description: Financed the Renaissance through banking innovation

events.eventText: The Dutch East India Company issues shares
events.description: The first company to offer public stock ownership

events.eventText: The South Sea Bubble bursts
events.description: Even Isaac Newton lost a fortune in the crash

events.eventText: Adam Smith publishes The Wealth of Nations
events.description: Published the same year as American independence

events.eventText: The Wall Street Crash begins
events.description: The Dow didn't recover its pre-crash high until 1954

events.eventText: The Bretton Woods Agreement is signed
events.description: 44 nations pegged their currencies to the US dollar

events.eventText: Deng Xiaoping launches China's economic reforms
events.description: Lifted more people out of poverty than any policy ever

events.eventText: Lehman Brothers collapses
events.description: The largest bankruptcy in US history

events.eventText: Bitcoin's genesis block is mined
events.description: Created by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto

events.eventText: Andreas Vesalius publishes modern anatomy
events.description: Corrected errors Galen had made studying animals

events.eventText: James Lind tests citrus against scurvy
events.description: The Navy didn't adopt his advice for 42 years

events.eventText: Ether is first used in surgery at Mass General
events.description: A dentist demonstrated it to a stunned audience

events.eventText: Louis Pasteur demonstrates pasteurisation
events.description: Originally designed to save the French wine industry

events.eventText: Robert Koch identifies the TB bacterium
events.description: TB was killing one in seven Europeans at the time

events.eventText: Karl Landsteiner discovers blood groups
events.description: Made safe blood transfusions possible

events.eventText: The first successful kidney transplant
events.description: Between identical twins at a Boston hospital

events.eventText: The WHO declares smallpox eradicated
events.description: The only human disease ever completely wiped out

events.eventText: The first antiretroviral for HIV is approved
events.description: AZT changed HIV from a death sentence to treatable

events.eventText: The first mRNA COVID vaccine is administered
events.description: Developed in under a year — a record for any vaccine

events.eventText: Cyrus the Great issues the Cyrus Cylinder
events.description: Sometimes called the first declaration of human rights

events.eventText: King John seals the Magna Carta
events.description: No free man shall be imprisoned except by lawful judgment

events.eventText: The English Bill of Rights is enacted
events.description: Banned cruel punishment and established parliamentary power

events.eventText: France adopts the Rights of Man declaration
events.description: Declared that men are born and remain free and equal

events.eventText: Britain abolishes slavery across its Empire
events.description: Campaigned for decades by Wilberforce and others

events.eventText: New Zealand gives women the right to vote
events.description: Led by Kate Sheppard's petition of 32,000 signatures

events.eventText: The UN adopts the Universal Declaration of Human Rights
events.description: Drafted by a committee chaired by Eleanor Roosevelt

events.eventText: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her bus seat
events.description: Sparked the 381-day Montgomery bus boycott

events.eventText: The US Civil Rights Act becomes law
events.description: Outlawed discrimination by race, colour, religion, or sex

events.eventText: Nelson Mandela is elected President
events.description: South Africa's first fully democratic election

events.eventText: The Colosseum opens in Rome
events.description: Could seat 50,000 and had a retractable shade awning

events.eventText: Hagia Sophia is consecrated in Constantinople
events.description: Its dome remained the world's largest for 1,000 years

events.eventText: The Leaning Tower of Pisa starts to tilt
events.description: Soft ground caused it to lean during construction

events.eventText: The Taj Mahal is completed in Agra
events.description: 20,000 workers built it as a memorial to a wife

events.eventText: The Eiffel Tower opens for the Paris World Fair
events.description: Originally planned to be dismantled after 20 years

events.eventText: The Panama Canal opens to shipping
events.description: Cut the New York-to-San Francisco route by 12,000 km

events.eventText: The Empire State Building is completed
events.description: Built in just 410 days during the Great Depression

events.eventText: The Sydney Opera House officially opens
events.description: Took 16 years to build — 10 years over schedule

events.eventText: The Channel Tunnel opens
events.description: 31 miles of tunnel under the English Channel

events.eventText: The Burj Khalifa opens in Dubai
events.description: At 828m, nearly twice the height of the Empire State

events.eventText: Chinese astronomers record a daytime supernova
events.description: The remnant is now the Crab Nebula

events.eventText: Galileo builds his first telescope
events.description: Could magnify objects roughly 8 times

events.eventText: William Herschel discovers Uranus
events.description: Initially thought it was a comet

events.eventText: Robert Goddard launches the first liquid rocket
events.description: It flew for just 2.5 seconds

events.eventText: Sputnik begins orbiting the Earth
events.description: Its beeps could be heard by amateur radio worldwide

events.eventText: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first person in space
events.description: His flight lasted exactly 108 minutes

events.eventText: Apollo 11 astronauts land on the Moon
events.description: Armstrong and Aldrin walked for just 2.5 hours

events.eventText: The Hubble Space Telescope is deployed
events.description: Its main mirror was initially ground to the wrong shape

events.eventText: The ISS receives its first resident crew
events.description: Has been continuously occupied for over 25 years

events.eventText: Perseverance rover lands on Mars
events.description: Carried a helicopter that flew on another planet

events.eventText: Rice cultivation begins in the Yangtze valley
events.description: Now feeds more than half the world's population

events.eventText: Romans build the Appian Aqueduct
events.description: Enabled farming far from natural water sources

events.eventText: Coffee beans are first brewed in Ethiopia
events.description: A goatherd noticed his goats dancing after eating berries

events.eventText: Columbus brings the first crops back to Europe
events.description: Tomatoes, potatoes, and chocolate crossed the Atlantic

events.eventText: Frederick the Great orders Prussians to grow potatoes
events.description: Potatoes became a European staple within decades

events.eventText: Nicolas Appert invents canned food
events.description: Developed to feed Napoleon's army on campaigns

events.eventText: Mendel publishes his laws of inheritance
events.description: Studied 28,000 pea plants in a monastery garden

events.eventText: Norman Borlaug releases high-yield wheat
events.description: His Green Revolution saved an estimated billion lives

events.eventText: The first genetically modified crop is planted
events.description: The Flavr Savr tomato arrived in US supermarkets

events.eventText: The first lab-grown burger is eaten publicly
events.description: Cost 215,000 pounds to produce a single patty

events.eventText: The Phoenician alphabet is created
events.description: Ancestor of nearly every alphabet used today

events.eventText: The Rosetta Stone is carved in Egypt
events.description: Its three scripts later unlocked hieroglyphs

events.eventText: Paper reaches Samarkand via Chinese prisoners
events.description: Captured papermakers taught the technique

events.eventText: The first printed newspaper appears in Germany
events.description: Called Relation, it covered news from across Europe

events.eventText: Samuel Morse sends the first telegraph message
events.description: What hath God wrought — Washington to Baltimore

events.eventText: Marconi sends a radio signal across the Atlantic
events.description: Morse code from Cornwall to Newfoundland

events.eventText: The BBC begins regular TV broadcasts
events.description: Launched from Alexandra Palace in London

events.eventText: The first email is sent between two computers
events.description: The machines were sitting side by side

events.eventText: Facebook launches from a Harvard dorm room
events.description: Had one million users within its first year

events.eventText: Twitter sends its first ever tweet
events.description: Jack Dorsey posted: just setting up my twttr

events.eventText: Greek tragedy debuts at the Dionysia festival
events.description: Actors wore masks so distant audiences could read emotions

events.eventText: The first opera, Dafne, premieres in Florence
events.description: Most of the original music has since been lost

events.eventText: Mozart composes his first symphony aged eight
events.description: Wrote over 600 works before dying at 35

events.eventText: Thomas Edison patents the phonograph
events.description: The first recording was Mary Had a Little Lamb

events.eventText: Robert Johnson records his blues sessions
events.description: Only 29 songs but influenced all of rock music

events.eventText: The Beatles appear on The Ed Sullivan Show
events.description: Watched by 73 million Americans — a record

events.eventText: Live Aid broadcasts to 1.9 billion people
events.description: Performed simultaneously in London and Philadelphia

events.eventText: Napster launches and disrupts the music industry
events.description: Peaked at 80 million users before being shut down

events.eventText: YouTube uploads its first ever video
events.description: Me at the zoo — 19 seconds at the San Diego Zoo

events.eventText: BTS tops the US charts with Dynamite
events.description: First K-pop act to debut at number one on Billboard

events.eventText: Cleopatra VII becomes Queen of Egypt
events.description: Spoke nine languages; first Ptolemy to learn Egyptian

events.eventText: Wu Zetian declares herself Empress of China
events.description: The only woman to hold the title Empress regnant

events.eventText: Joan of Arc lifts the siege of Orleans
events.description: She was just 17 years old

events.eventText: Mary Wollstonecraft publishes Rights of Woman
events.description: Argued women were not inferior, just less educated

events.eventText: Florence Nightingale arrives at Scutari
events.description: Reduced the death rate from 42% to 2%

events.eventText: Marie Curie wins her first Nobel Prize
events.description: First woman to win, later the first person to win two

events.eventText: Amelia Earhart flies solo across the Atlantic
events.description: Completed the crossing in under 15 hours

events.eventText: Rosa Parks is arrested on a Montgomery bus
events.description: Sparked the 381-day Montgomery bus boycott

events.eventText: Valentina Tereshkova orbits Earth
events.description: First woman in space — orbited 48 times in 3 days

events.eventText: Malala Yousafzai wins the Nobel Peace Prize
events.description: At 17, the youngest Nobel laureate in history

events.eventText: Sun Tzu completes The Art of War
events.description: Still studied at military academies worldwide

events.eventText: Emperor Qin unifies China
events.description: Standardised weights, measures, and writing

events.eventText: The Tang Dynasty is proclaimed in China
events.description: Ushered in a golden age of poetry, art, and trade

events.eventText: A Mongol invasion fleet sinks off Japan
events.description: A typhoon — later called kamikaze — destroyed it

events.eventText: Zheng He launches his first treasure voyage
events.description: His ships were five times the size of Columbus's

events.eventText: Perry's ships arrive in Tokyo Bay
events.description: Forced Japan open to trade after 200 years of isolation

events.eventText: The Meiji Emperor takes power in Japan
events.description: Transformed Japan from feudal state to industrial power

events.eventText: Mao proclaims the People's Republic of China
events.description: Declared from the Gate of Heavenly Peace in Beijing

events.eventText: Tiananmen Square protests are suppressed
events.description: The iconic Tank Man photo was seen worldwide

events.eventText: Tokyo hosts the delayed 2020 Olympics
events.description: Held without spectators due to the pandemic

events.eventText: The Olmecs erect colossal stone heads
events.description: Some weigh up to 50 tonnes

events.eventText: Tenochtitlan falls to Hernan Cortes
events.description: Smallpox killed far more Aztecs than Spanish weapons

events.eventText: The Mayflower lands at Plymouth Rock
events.description: Only about half the passengers survived the first winter

events.eventText: The US Constitution is ratified
events.description: The oldest written national constitution still in use

events.eventText: Bolivar wins the Battle of Boyaca
events.description: Secured independence for what became Colombia

events.eventText: Brazil abolishes slavery
events.description: The last country in the Western Hemisphere to do so

events.eventText: The Panama Canal opens to shipping
events.description: Over 25,000 workers died during its construction

events.eventText: The Cuban Missile Crisis peaks
events.description: For 13 days, nuclear war seemed imminent

events.eventText: Pinochet seizes power in a Chilean coup
events.description: President Allende died during the assault on the palace

events.eventText: Barack Obama is inaugurated as US President
events.description: The first Black president of the United States

events.eventText: The Kingdom of Kush conquers Egypt
events.description: Nubian pharaohs ruled Egypt for nearly a century

events.eventText: The Library of Alexandria is founded
events.description: Aimed to contain a copy of every book in the world

events.eventText: The Kingdom of Aksum adopts Christianity
events.description: One of the first states in the world to do so

events.eventText: Mansa Musa arrives in Cairo on his hajj
events.description: Gave away so much gold he crashed Egypt's economy

events.eventText: Great Zimbabwe reaches its peak population
events.description: Stone walls without mortar housed 18,000 people

events.eventText: The Zulu Kingdom rises under Shaka
events.description: Revolutionised warfare with the short stabbing spear

events.eventText: The Berlin Conference partitions Africa
events.description: Not a single African leader was invited

events.eventText: Ghana gains independence from Britain
events.description: Kwame Nkrumah inspired movements across the continent

events.eventText: The Rwandan genocide begins
events.description: Approximately 800,000 people killed in 100 days

events.eventText: The African Union is established
events.description: Replaced the Organisation of African Unity

events.eventText: Romans complete the Appian Way
events.description: Sections of the original road are still walkable

events.eventText: Viking longships cross the North Atlantic
events.description: Could sail in water just one metre deep

events.eventText: A sheep, duck, and rooster fly in a balloon
events.description: The first hot air balloon passengers in Paris

events.eventText: The Liverpool-Manchester railway opens
events.description: A politician was killed by a train at the ceremony

events.eventText: Karl Benz patents the first automobile
events.description: Had three wheels and a top speed of 16 km/h

events.eventText: The Wright Brothers fly at Kitty Hawk
events.description: Covered less distance than a 747's wingspan

events.eventText: Alcock and Brown fly non-stop across the Atlantic
events.description: Crash-landed in an Irish bog

events.eventText: Concorde makes its first commercial flight
events.description: Crossed the Atlantic in under 3.5 hours

events.eventText: Eurostar begins service through the Channel Tunnel
events.description: London to Paris in just over 3 hours

events.eventText: SpaceX Falcon 9 lands a booster upright
events.description: Made reusable orbital rockets a reality

events.eventText: Hammurabi's Code is carved in Babylon
events.description: One of the earliest written legal codes: 282 laws

events.eventText: Athens introduces democratic voting
events.description: Citizens voted directly on laws — no representatives

events.eventText: The Roman Twelve Tables are displayed publicly
events.description: So all citizens could read the law for themselves

events.eventText: Justinian codifies Roman law
events.description: The foundation of most European legal systems

events.eventText: The Treaty of Westphalia is signed
events.description: Established the modern concept of state sovereignty

events.eventText: The Napoleonic Code is enacted
events.description: Influenced legal systems across Europe and Latin America

events.eventText: The League of Nations holds its first session
events.description: Failed to prevent war but paved the way for the UN

events.eventText: The United Nations is founded in San Francisco
events.description: 51 countries signed the charter

events.eventText: The European Union is formally created
events.description: The Maastricht Treaty took effect on 1 November

events.eventText: The ICC holds its first trial at The Hague
events.description: Tried a Congolese warlord for using child soldiers

events.eventText: Windmills are first used in Persia
events.description: Vertical-axis mills ground grain and pumped water

events.eventText: Coal mining begins at scale in England
events.description: London's air was already notoriously smoky

events.eventText: James Watt patents an improved steam engine
events.description: Made steam power efficient enough for factories

events.eventText: Edwin Drake drills the first oil well
events.description: Launched the petroleum age in Pennsylvania

events.eventText: Edison opens the first power station
events.description: Lit up a square mile of lower Manhattan

events.eventText: Fermi achieves a nuclear chain reaction
events.description: Under the stands of a Chicago football stadium

events.eventText: OPEC imposes an oil embargo on the West
events.description: Quadrupled oil prices and caused global recession

events.eventText: The Three Gorges Dam begins generating power
events.description: The world's largest power station by installed capacity

events.eventText: Fukushima's reactors melt down after a tsunami
events.description: The worst nuclear disaster since Chernobyl

events.eventText: A fusion reactor produces net energy gain
events.description: Achieved at the US National Ignition Facility

events.eventText: The ancient Olympic Games are first held
events.description: Just a single footrace for the first 13 games

events.eventText: The rules of golf are written in Scotland
events.description: Just 13 rules covered the entire game

events.eventText: The first modern Olympics are held in Athens
events.description: Just 241 athletes from 14 nations competed

events.eventText: The first football World Cup kicks off
events.description: Several European teams refused to make the journey

events.eventText: Jesse Owens wins four golds in Berlin
events.description: Defied Hitler's vision of Aryan supremacy

events.eventText: Roger Bannister runs a sub-four-minute mile
events.description: His record lasted just 46 days

events.eventText: Ali defeats Foreman in the Rumble in the Jungle
events.description: Watched by 60,000 fans in Kinshasa

events.eventText: Usain Bolt sets the 100m world record
events.description: 9.58 seconds — a record that still stands

events.eventText: Leicester City win the Premier League
events.description: Defied 5,000-to-1 odds to become champions

events.eventText: Messi wins the World Cup with Argentina
events.description: Considered the final piece of his legacy

events.eventText: The Epic of Gilgamesh is inscribed on tablets
events.description: The oldest known work of literature

events.eventText: Nebuchadnezzar II builds the Ishtar Gate
events.description: Decorated with 575 glazed-brick dragons and bulls

events.eventText: The House of Wisdom opens in Baghdad
events.description: Translated Greek, Persian, and Indian texts

events.eventText: Crusaders storm Jerusalem
events.description: The First Crusade ended with a brutal sack of the city

events.eventText: Suleiman the Magnificent takes power
events.description: Expanded the Ottoman Empire to its greatest extent

events.eventText: The Suez Canal opens to shipping
events.description: Reduced the London-to-Mumbai route by 7,000 km

events.eventText: Oil is discovered at Dammam in Saudi Arabia
events.description: Transformed the kingdom from poor to rich

events.eventText: Israel declares independence
events.description: Recognised by the US within 11 minutes

events.eventText: The Camp David Accords are signed
events.description: First peace treaty between Israel and an Arab state

events.eventText: The Burj Khalifa opens
events.description: At 828 metres tall, visible from 95 km away

events.eventText: The Plague of Athens strikes the city
events.description: Killed up to a quarter of the Athenian army

events.eventText: Plague of Justinian reaches Constantinople
events.description: The first recorded pandemic of bubonic plague

events.eventText: Black Death arrives in Sicily on trading ships
events.description: Killed 30-60% of Europe's population in five years

events.eventText: Smallpox devastates the Aztec capital
events.description: Killed millions and enabled the Spanish conquest

events.eventText: The Great Plague kills 68,000 in London
events.description: Bills of mortality tracked the weekly death toll

events.eventText: John Snow traces cholera to a Soho water pump
events.description: Founded the science of epidemiology

events.eventText: Spanish Flu kills 50-100 million worldwide
events.description: More deadly than the entire First World War

events.eventText: The first cases of HIV/AIDS are identified
events.description: Has since killed over 40 million people

events.eventText: SARS spreads from Guangdong to 29 countries
events.description: Contained within months through aggressive quarantine

events.eventText: The WHO declares COVID-19 a global pandemic
events.description: Led to the fastest vaccine development ever

events.eventText: Julius Caesar uses a substitution cipher
events.description: Shifted each letter three places in the alphabet

events.eventText: Walsingham uncovers the Babington Plot
events.description: Intercepted coded letters condemning Mary Queen of Scots

events.eventText: Nathan Hale is hanged as an American spy
events.description: I only regret that I have but one life to lose

events.eventText: Mata Hari is executed by firing squad
events.description: Whether she was actually a spy remains debated

events.eventText: Turing's team cracks the Enigma code
events.description: Estimated to have shortened the war by two years

events.eventText: The CIA is formally established
events.description: Grew out of the wartime Office of Strategic Services

events.eventText: Gary Powers' U-2 spy plane is shot down
events.description: He survived and was exchanged for a Soviet spy

events.eventText: The Watergate break-in is discovered
events.description: Led to the only resignation of a US president

events.eventText: The Mitrokhin Archive of KGB files is revealed
events.description: A defector smuggled 25,000 pages out of Moscow

events.eventText: Edward Snowden leaks NSA documents
events.description: Revealed mass surveillance of millions of citizens

events.eventText: Gutenberg builds his printing press
events.description: Within 50 years, 20 million volumes had been printed

events.eventText: Copernicus publishes his heliocentric model
events.description: Published the same year as Vesalius's anatomy book

events.eventText: Newcomen builds the first practical steam engine
events.description: Used to pump water out of coal mines

events.eventText: Niepce captures the first photograph
events.description: Required an eight-hour exposure

events.eventText: Einstein publishes general relativity
events.description: Predicted gravity bends light — confirmed four years later

events.eventText: Colossus, the first programmable computer, runs
events.description: Built at Bletchley Park to break German codes

events.eventText: The transistor is invented at Bell Labs
events.description: The tiny switch behind all modern electronics

events.eventText: ARPANET sends its first message
events.description: The system crashed after transmitting just LO

events.eventText: The Human Genome Project is completed
events.description: Mapped roughly 3 billion base pairs of DNA

events.eventText: AlphaGo defeats world champion Lee Sedol
events.description: Go was considered too complex for computers
